summary: Power outages spread across Zaporizhzhia region as Russian forces threaten with Iskander-M ballistic launches from multiple directions. Ukrainian defenses report intensified UAV attacks on Kharkiv, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, while Russian infiltration near Pokrovsk creates a contested pocket. Diplomatic moves continue, but frontline combat remains active across eastern, northern and southern sectors.
key_points: Iskander-M ballistic missile launches threatened from Taganrog (Rostov), Bryansk and Bratsk regions, prompting nationwide alert (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839, @mon1tor_ua/66836).
Ukrainian power grid suffered emergency outages in large parts of Zaporizhzhia oblast, disrupting civilian infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/376606).
Russian UAVs from the Rubicon unit penetrated deep behind Ukrainian lines, shooting down Ukrainian drones and using dual‑camera systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Intense fighting reported near Pokrovsk northern outskirts, with Russian units infiltrating villages Shchevchenko and threatening supply routes (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).
Kharkiv suffered two strikes in Novobavarsky district, killing three civilians and damaging 25 homes; additional UAV strike hit a warehouse in Izmail district (Source: @UkraineNow/81303).
Multiple UAV swarms reported over Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions, including Shahed attacks north of Kremenchuk and over Kryvyi Rih (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66834).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted two UAE drones; a third drone struck a generator near the Baraka nuclear plant (Source: @tass_agency/376611).
Russian forces launched KAB rockets in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk oblasts, adding to artillery pressure on frontline positions (Source: @kpszsu/62249).

sections.executive_summary: Iskander-M ballistic missile threats from Taganrog, Bryansk and Bratsk have heightened strategic risk across Ukraine. Power outages cripple Zaporizhzhia region while Russian UAVs and infiltrations intensify combat near Pokrovsk and Kharkiv. Frontline clashes continue across eastern, northern and southern sectors, with both sides sustaining casualties and material losses.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported emergency electricity shutdowns affecting most of Zaporizhzhia oblast, forcing hospitals and industrial sites to rely on backup generators (Source: @tass_agency/376606).

The Ukrainian military confirmed that the Rubicon UAV unit is conducting deep‑strike missions, destroying Ukrainian reconnaissance drones and employing dual‑sensor payloads to evade detection (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30272).

In Kharkiv, two Russian strikes hit the Novobavarsky district, killing three civilians, damaging 25 private houses and one high‑rise building; a separate UAV hit a warehouse roof in Izmail district, causing a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81303).

Ukrainian air defenses intercepted two UAE‑supplied drones, though a third drone impacted a generator near the Baraka nuclear power plant, raising concerns about critical infrastructure safety (Source: @tass_agency/376611).
Ukrainian forces reported successful repulsion of KAB rocket salvos in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk, limiting Russian artillery impact on frontline positions (Source: @kpszsu/62249).

Russian forces announced readiness to launch Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Taganrog (Rostov), Bryansk and Bratsk regions, signaling a potential escalation in long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839, @mon1tor_ua/66836).
The Rubicon UAV unit is actively targeting Ukrainian rear areas, employing upgraded drones with day and thermal cameras to increase strike accuracy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30272).

Russian infantry and special‑operations units have infiltrated north of Pokrovsk, establishing a pocket that threatens supply lines to the city of Shchevchenko and creates a “gray zone” for further advances (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).
Russian KAB rockets were launched across Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk oblasts, adding pressure on Ukrainian defensive positions and civilian settlements (Source: @kpszsu/62249).

Russian air assets, including MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, were observed taking off from Savasiyskoye airfield, indicating a potential high‑speed strike capability in the region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66835).

Intense combat continues around Pokrovsk, with Russian units infiltrating villages Shchevchenko and threatening the supply corridor to the city; Ukrainian forces report increased UAV activity over the area (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).

Ukrainian reports indicate Russian pressure on Riznyky and Kalynky, with attempts to break through to rear positions near Ray‑Oleksandrivka, exploiting Ukrainian manpower shortages (Source: @DeepStateUA/23518).
UAV swarms were observed over Donetsk, targeting Sloviansk, Kramatorsk and Bilitske, complicating Ukrainian air defense and logistics (Source: @eRadarrua/92660).
Russian artillery and KAB rocket fire hit positions near Mar'kove and Novomarkove, causing civilian casualties and damaging infrastructure (Source: @DeepStateUA/23518).
Ukrainian forces claim successful defense of Zakytne and Ozernoye, repelling Russian assaults despite heavy UAV and artillery pressure (Source: @DeepStateUA/23518).
Russian pilots have established a foothold north of Pokrovsk, controlling urban areas and using UAVs to disrupt Ukrainian logistics, according to frontline reports (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).
Ukrainian intelligence notes increased Russian infiltration attempts toward the village of Shevchenko, aiming to create a corridor toward the northern supply routes (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).
UAV activity reported north of Chernihiv, heading toward Snovsk, indicating Russian reconnaissance focus on northern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62229).
No major ground offensives reported, but Russian forces maintain pressure on northern approaches to Pokrovsk, complicating Ukrainian reinforcement efforts (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted several low‑altitude UAVs over the northern sector, limiting Russian ISR capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/62260).
Russian UAV attacks continued over southern Odesa region, with multiple strikes on civilian infrastructure and logistics hubs (Source: @UkraineNow/81303).
Ukrainian forces reported Russian KAB rocket launches targeting positions in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, increasing pressure on southern defensive lines (Source: @kpszsu/62249).
Power outages in Zaporizhzhia oblast have hampered civilian resilience and may affect Ukrainian command‑and‑control capabilities in the south (Source: @tass_agency/376606).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted a UAV that struck a generator near the Baraka nuclear plant, highlighting vulnerability of critical energy assets in the south (Source: @tass_agency/376611).
Russian artillery fire reported near the southern town of Mykolaiv, with shelling causing minor damage to civilian structures (Source: @kpszsu/62251).
Ukrainian forces shot down multiple Russian UAVs, including Rubicon‑equipped drones with dual cameras, reducing Russian ISR penetration (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30272).

Two UAE‑supplied drones were intercepted by Ukrainian air defense; a third drone hit a generator near Baraka nuclear plant, raising concerns over foreign‑supplied UAV effectiveness (Source: @tass_agency/376611).

Russian MiG‑31K fighters, capable of carrying Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, were observed taking off from Savasiyskoye, indicating potential high‑speed strike missions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66835).
Ukrainian air defenses reported successful interception of Shahed drones over Dnipropetrovsk, Kryvyi Rih and north of Kremenchuk, limiting Russian strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66834).
Russian KAB rocket launches across Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk were partially neutralized by Ukrainian air defense, preventing larger scale damage (Source: @kpszsu/62249).

key_developments: Iskander‑M ballistic missiles are poised for launch from Taganrog, Bryansk and Bratsk, raising strategic strike risk across Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839).
Emergency power outages cripple large parts of Zaporizhzhia oblast, affecting civilian and military infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/376606).
Russian Rubicon UAV unit conducts deep‑strike missions, destroying Ukrainian drones and employing dual‑sensor payloads (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Russian infiltration north of Pokrovsk creates a contested pocket, threatening supply routes to Shchevchenko (Source: @DeepStateUA/23517).
Kharkiv suffers civilian casualties and housing damage from two Russian strikes in Novobavarsky district (Source: @UkraineNow/81303).
Ukrainian air defenses intercept two UAE drones; a third drone hits a generator near Baraka nuclear plant (Source: @tass_agency/376611).
Multiple Shahed drone attacks reported north of Kremenchuk and over Kryvyi Rih, stressing Ukrainian air defense (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66834).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating long‑range strike capabilities with Iskander‑M missiles while intensifying UAV operations and ground infiltration near Pokrovsk, creating multi‑dimensional pressure on Ukrainian defenses. Ukrainian power infrastructure vulnerabilities in Zaporizhzhia compound operational challenges, but robust air defense interceptions have limited some UAV and rocket impacts. Continued Russian artillery and KAB rocket use across the south and east sustains a high‑intensity conflict environment.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have tightened export controls, exemplified by the UK ban on Russian uranium, while diplomatic engagements in Beijing continue without clear impact on battlefield dynamics. Ukrainian authorities are mobilizing civil defense measures to address power outages and civilian protection in affected oblasts. Russian diplomatic visits to China underscore a search for strategic backing amid mounting military pressures.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iskander‑M launch sites should prioritize satellite and SIGINT assets in Taganrog, Bryansk and Bratsk to pre‑empt missile launches. UAV infiltration routes near Pokrovsk require enhanced electronic warfare and ground reconnaissance to disrupt Russian pocket formation. Continued assessment of Ukrainian power grid resilience is critical for maintaining command‑and‑control capabilities in the Zaporizhzhia sector.
